steps.title: Identify how both texts prove authorship
steps.content:
Both Graves and modern scholars identify Fletcher's authorship through style analysis.

Graves explicitly states that Fletcher's “distinct style in his other plays” is “sufficient evidence of Fletcher's authorship.” Modern scholars similarly determine authorship “on the basis of style,” attributing certain acts to Fletcher.

steps.title: Match the shared assumption to the choices
steps.content:
The correct answer states that Fletcher's writing has a unique, identifiable style—the shared premise both texts build on.

For Graves, Fletcher's distinct style proved authorship; for modern scholars, style analysis determined which acts he wrote.

The other choices are either contradicted because Graves disputes Shakespeare's role, completely unsupported, or unique to one text's argument because women depictions appear only in Graves's reasoning, not Text 2's style-based approach.

That confirms A is correct.
